fileViewer.showingCommitted: This file was deleted; showing the last committed version
fileViewer.truncated: Large file: showing a truncated preview
fileViewer.compactDiff: Large file: showing a compact diff
fileViewer.discardBody: You have unsaved changes. They'll be lost if you continue.

addRepo.description: Point RepoYeti at an existing repo, create a new one, or clone a remote.
addRepo.hintRegister: Index an existing git repo by its absolute path.
addRepo.hintClone: Clone a remote repository into a folder RepoYeti scans.
addRepo.hintLore: Clone a repository from a registered Lore server. Add servers in Settings → Lore servers.
addRepo.loreNoServers: No Lore servers registered yet. Add one in Settings → Lore servers.
addRepo.title: Add a repository
addRepo.scanHint: Already have repos on disk? Scan your folders to find them.

accounts.commitIdentityNote: By default switching changes authentication only. Link an account to a saved identity below and switching to it also sets the commit author (name + email) to match.
accounts.description: Switch the active GitHub account on this machine. The active account is what push, pull, and any tool using git's GitHub CLI credential helper (including AI agents) authenticate as. Managed through the GitHub CLI (gh).
accounts.empty: No GitHub accounts are logged in. Run gh auth login to add one.
accounts.linkEmpty: Add an identity above to link one
accounts.title: GitHub accounts
accounts.unavailableBody: Install the GitHub CLI (gh) and run gh auth login to switch accounts here.

bulk.hideTooltip: Hide every selected repository from the dashboard
bulk.removeTooltip: Remove every selected repository from RepoYeti

header.fetchAllNoRepos: There are no repositories to fetch yet
header.fetchAllNone: No repos with a remote to fetch
header.cleanupMissingTooltip: Remove repositories whose folder no longer exists on disk. This also drops manually pinned or cloned repos, not just auto-discovered ones.
header.cleanupMissingDone: Removed {count} missing repo | Removed {count} missing repos
header.cleanupMissingNone: No missing repositories to clean up
header.updateRestart: Restart RepoYeti to run the new code.
header.shutdownBody: Stop the local daemon and background checks. Launch RepoYeti again to start it.

identity.description: RepoYeti-managed git identities you can attach to repos. They are not imported automatically; SSH keys are referenced by path, never read or copied.

detected.empty: No Git config, GitHub CLI login, credential-manager account, or SSH keys detected yet.
detected.hint: Read from this machine (global/repo git config, SSH keys, GitHub CLI), not from any one folder. Use one to save it, or dismiss it to stop it re-appearing.
detected.title: Detected on this computer

firewall.title: Identity Firewall
firewall.description: Pin a repo path pattern to a required identity. A commit/push in a matching repo is hard-blocked (dashboard AND any connected agent over MCP) unless the repo's resolved identity matches.
firewall.empty: No rules yet. Every repo commits/pushes with whatever identity is assigned to it.
firewall.needIdentityFirst: Add a git identity first (under Identities above). A firewall rule pins repos to one of your saved identities.
firewall.saveFailed: Could not save the Identity Firewall rules
firewall.needsIdentity: Choose a required identity for every rule before saving

identity.title: Identities

optIn.title: Using more than one git identity?
optIn.description: Commit identities let you stamp a different author name/email per repository, for example work vs. personal. Most people only ever need one, so this stays out of the way until you turn it on.
optIn.activeNote: Shown because you turned identities on.

actions.pullTooltip: Fetch and merge remote changes into your branch
actions.pushTooltip: Upload local commits to the remote
actions.syncTooltip: Pull and push changes with the Lore server

badge.behindTooltip: Behind by {count} as of last fetch
badge.identityViolation: Identity Firewall: this repo requires "{name}" to commit/push, but the resolved identity doesn't match

changes.gripAriaResized: Resize changes list: custom height, press Delete to reset
changes.gripTitleResized: Drag or ↑/↓ to resize · double-click to reset height

commit.noAiKeyHint: Add a key in Settings → AI, or turn off AI commit messages there.

smartCommit.buttonTitle: Let AI split your changes into separate, scoped commits
smartCommit.buttonTitleYolo: YOLO: split and commit immediately, no review
smartCommit.title: Smart commit
smartCommit.subtitle: {name}: group these changes into clean, scoped commits.
smartCommit.redraftBody: Re-drafting will replace your edited messages and file grouping with a fresh AI plan. This can't be undone.
smartCommit.degraded: The AI didn't return a usable plan, so these are grouped by folder. Edit them, or hit Regenerate to try again.
smartCommit.degradedRateLimited: Your AI provider's rate limit is used up, so these are grouped by folder instead. Edit them and commit as-is, switch provider in Settings, or retry later.
smartCommit.degradedUnreachable: Couldn't reach your AI provider, so these are grouped by folder. Check your connection, then hit Regenerate.
smartCommit.truncated: Large change set. The AI grouped from the full file list but only saw part of the diff, so double-check the messages.
smartCommit.unassignedHint: Put every file into a commit before committing.
smartCommit.diffTooLarge: Large file: tap the file above to view its diff

repo.toastAutoCommitOnGlobalOff: Auto-commit on for this repo. It starts running once you switch Auto-commit on in Settings.

err.detachedHead: Detached HEAD. Resolve at your desk.
err.dirtyWorkingTree: Uncommitted changes. Resolve at your desk.
err.wouldOverwrite: Your uncommitted changes would be overwritten. Commit or stash them first.
err.noUpstream: This branch has no upstream set.
err.nonFastForward: Remote has diverged. Resolve at your desk.
err.sshAuthFailed: Authentication failed. Check this repo's identity / SSH key.
err.sshPassphraseRequired: SSH key needs a passphrase. Use ssh-agent or a passphrase-free key.
err.branchExists: A branch with that name already exists.
err.unmergedBranch: This branch isn't fully merged. Delete it at your desk.
err.cannotDeleteCurrent: You can't delete the branch you're on.
err.protectedBranch: That branch is protected. Delete it at your desk.
err.nothingToStash: Nothing to stash: the working tree is clean.
err.stashConflict: Stash applied with conflicts. Resolve at your desk (the stash was kept).

branches.createTooltip: Create a new branch from here

history.title: History
history.detailUnavailable: Commit detail isn't available for this repository.
history.author: Author

discard.title: Discard changes?
discard.body: Discard all changes to {file}? This reverts it to the last commit and can't be undone.

manage.title: Remote & tags
manage.remoteHint: Set where this repo pushes and pulls. Updating origin is a local git-config change, no network.

syncAccount.dropdownLabel: GitHub account to sync this repo as
syncAccount.setTitle: Choose the GitHub account this repo syncs as

rename.title: Rename repository
rename.description: This changes the label shown in RepoYeti only. The folder on your disk keeps its own name; nothing is moved or renamed. Leave it empty to go back to the folder name.

remove.title: Remove "{name}"?
remove.description: This removes the repository from RepoYeti's list only. Your folder, your files and your git history are not touched; nothing is deleted from disk. RepoYeti will also stop re-adding it on future scans. You can undo this, or restore it later from Settings.

repo.toastRenameCleared: Label cleared: showing the folder name

preview.title: Preview pull
preview.noUpstream: This branch isn't tracking a remote branch, so there's nothing to pull.
preview.upToDate: Already up to date. Nothing to pull.
preview.commitsTruncated: Only the most recent commits are listed.
preview.filesTruncated: Only the first files are listed; the totals above cover all of them.
preview.cleanMerge: Merges cleanly with your local commits.
preview.willConflict: Would conflict in {count} file | Would conflict in {count} files
preview.conflictUnknown: Couldn't test the merge on this version of git.

remote.description: Reach RepoYeti from your phone: toggle remote on, scan the code, and sign in with Connections.
remote.localBody: RepoYeti is only reachable on this computer. Turn on remote access to open it from your phone; you'll sign in with Connections.
remote.localBypassActive: Using RepoYeti locally without signing in
remote.needsOwner: Remote access needs a Connections account: sign in once to claim this RepoYeti, then flip the toggle again.
remote.scanHint: Scan with your phone's camera to open RepoYeti there.
remote.title: Remote access
remote.editHint: When off, files can only be edited from this computer (loopback).
remote.shareHint: Need to share just one repo? A share link gives someone a scoped, expiring view without handing over your whole dashboard.

notify.behindOneBody: {count} commit behind its remote | {count} commits behind its remote
notify.behindManyBody: {count} repo has new remote commits | {count} repos have new remote commits
notify.behindPullSomeFailed: Pull failed for {count} repo | Pull failed for {count} repos
notify.autoCommitDegradedNote: AI was unavailable, so these were split by the built-in grouping
notify.autoCommitDegradedSomeNote: AI was unavailable for {n} of them, which used the built-in grouping | AI was unavailable for {n} of them, which used the built-in grouping
notify.autoCommitBlockedBody: {name} was skipped: resolve it at your desk
notify.autoCommitBlockedManyBody: {count} repo was skipped: resolve it at your desk | {count} repos were skipped: resolve them at your desk
notify.autoCommitBlockedAiBody: {name} was skipped: your AI provider was unavailable. Nothing was lost; the next run retries.
notify.autoCommitBlockedAiManyBody: {count} repo was skipped: your AI provider was unavailable. Nothing was lost; the next run retries. | {count} repos were skipped: your AI provider was unavailable. Nothing was lost; the next run retries.
notify.newProjectsBody: {count} new project found | {count} new projects found
notify.aiKeyInvalidBody: {provider} rejected your API key. Update it in Settings → AI.
notify.updateBody: A newer version of RepoYeti is ready to install.
notify.updatePromptBody: A newer version of RepoYeti is ready. Installing takes a few seconds and restarts the app.
notify.updateBlockedBody: A newer version is ready, but it can't be installed right now.

scan.title: Scan for projects
scan.description: Find Git repositories on this computer.
scan.machineHint: Scans every drive for Git repositories. New ones are added and watched automatically.
scan.folderHint: Scans just this folder and everything under it.

settings.aiDescription: Bring your own key for any provider. Groq is the suggested one (free + fast, ~30s to set up). RepoYeti uses the key on this machine only; it never leaves the daemon, and generating a message sends the repo's diff to the selected provider.
settings.aiCommitEnableHint: Show the ✨ Generate and Auto buttons on repo cards. On even without a key: clicking one then prompts you to add a key. Turn off to hide them entirely.
settings.aiCommitEnableFailed: Could not change AI commit messages
settings.aiYoloHint: Skip the review screen: generate the plan and commit it immediately. Never auto-pushes.
settings.aiStyleHint: How AI-generated commit messages are phrased.
settings.aiDiffDetailHint: How much of each changed file the AI reads before the rest is folded away. Applies to both ✨ Generate and Auto. It always sees the full file list and every file's +/- stat, so smart-commit grouping is unaffected; this only trades how much of a large file feeds its message against tokens spent per commit. Leaner means more commits per day on a rate-limited key. Lean ≈ 30 changed lines per file, Balanced ≈ 50, Thorough ≈ 100.
settings.freeTierHint: This provider offers a free API tier, so you can use it at no cost. It's a signpost about the provider, not your key or plan; you still add your own key above.
settings.suggestedNudge: Free and fast. Grab a key (~30 seconds), then paste it below:
settings.signOutAllHint: Invalidate the session on every device. You'll need to sign in again.
settings.tunnelHint: By default your phone link changes on every restart. Add a Cloudflare named tunnel (a hostname and connector token) for a permanent address that resolves on any network.
settings.tunnelTokenSaved: Token saved: leave blank to keep it
settings.tunnelTokenEnv: The token is set by the CF_TUNNEL_TOKEN environment variable.
settings.rootsHint: Folders RepoYeti scans for git repositories. Adding one scans it right away; removing one drops the repos found under it.
settings.rootsRemoved: Removed: {count} repo dropped | Removed: {count} repos dropped
settings.serversHint: Lore servers RepoYeti can clone repos from. Auth is handled by the Lore CLI (lore login); no credentials are stored here.
settings.serversIpHint: Tip: use an IP address (e.g. 127.0.0.1) instead of localhost; a localhost URL can stall ~30s on first connect.
settings.editorHint: Which editor the file viewer's "Open with" button launches. Editors run on this computer, so it only works when you're using RepoYeti locally.

cloudSync.title: Sync settings
cloudSync.privacyNote: Syncs theme & preferences to your Connections account; optional; never syncs secrets or file paths.
cloudSync.enableHint: Keep your theme and preferences the same across every device signed in with Connections.

settings.hotkeysEnableHint: Master switch for every app shortcut.
settings.hotkeysPowerHint: Adds Ctrl/⌘+Enter to commit from the message box.
settings.changesHeightHint: How tall each repo's changed-files list may grow before it scrolls. The list is only as tall as its contents, up to this. Drag the grip under a list to cap that repo lower; double-click the grip to reset it.
settings.showTooltipsHint: Hover help on buttons and controls. Info icons like this one always stay on so settings remain explained.
settings.portableWindowHint: Open the app in its own window (no tabs or address bar) instead of a browser tab. The desktop launcher/tray follows this too.
settings.portableWindowOpened: Opened in portable window - you can close this tab
settings.portableWindowNoBrowser: No Edge or Chrome found for a portable window
settings.hideTrayIconHint: Hide the notification-area icon. RepoYeti keeps running in the background; launching the shortcut again reopens the UI, and you can re-enable the icon right here in Settings.
settings.hideTrayIconFailed: Could not change tray icon visibility
settings.description: Accounts, appearance, diff behavior, and AI commit messages. Changes apply instantly.
settings.diffStatsHint: Show added/removed line and character counts per file and per repo. Computed from each repo's diff, a small extra cost on every refresh.
settings.diffPatchThresholdHint: Files larger than this open as a compact patch (only the changed lines) instead of a full side-by-side diff, which is faster on big files.
settings.diffPatchThresholdFailed: Could not change the diff threshold
settings.diffPatchAlwaysHint: Never switch large files to the compact patch; always load the full side-by-side diff (very large files may be truncated).
settings.diffPatchAlwaysFailed: Could not change the diff view
settings.syncDescription: Quietly check your remotes so RepoYeti can warn you the moment a repo falls behind.
settings.syncCheckHint: Periodically fetch each repo so the dashboard knows when new commits land on the remote.
settings.syncCheckFailed: Could not change the sync check
settings.syncIntervalHint: How often to look for new remote commits.
settings.syncIntervalFailed: Could not change the check interval
settings.keepInSyncHint: When a repo falls behind, pull the new commits automatically, but only when it's a safe fast-forward (clean working tree, no local-only commits). Anything risky is left for you.
settings.autoCommitHint: The on/off switch for the whole feature. Turning it on by itself commits nothing; you also opt each repo in from its ⋯ menu. Only the repos you opt in are ever auto-committed.
settings.autoCommitPullHint: Fast-forward from the remote (pull --ff-only) before pushing, so an unattended run never publishes over a diverged remote.
settings.autoCommitPushHint: Push to the remote after each auto-commit. Turn off to commit locally only.
settings.autoCommitAiFallback: If AI can't split the commits
settings.autoCommitAiFallbackHint: Only applies once you've set an AI provider and it fails: quota, an outage, or a reply we can't read. With no provider set, the built-in grouping is always the planner and this has no effect.
settings.autoCommitAiFallbackSkip: Skip that run, retry next time
settings.autoCommitDescription: Commits your work on a schedule, in two steps: switch this on to arm the feature, then opt individual repos in from their ⋯ menu. Nothing you did not opt in is ever touched. A repo with a merge conflict, or mid-merge/rebase, is always skipped. Commits are split by your AI provider when one is set, otherwise by a simple built-in grouping.
settings.autoCommitNoReposYet: No repos are opted in yet. Open a repo's ⋯ menu and choose Auto-commit to include it; this switch stays armed but idle until then.
settings.autoCommitReposOptedIn: {n} repo is opted in (via its ⋯ menu). | {n} repos are opted in (via their ⋯ menus).
settings.autoUpdateHint: Install updates as soon as they're found, then restart the app. Off by default: it restarts the daemon unattended. A repo with uncommitted changes is never updated.
settings.mcpApprovalGateHint: Any AI agent connected over MCP (Claude Desktop/Code, Cursor, …) must wait for you to approve a commit, branch switch, push, pull, or fetch here in the dashboard before it runs. Read-only lookups are never gated.
settings.mcpAutoDenyHint: If you don't approve or deny in time, the request is denied automatically so the agent never hangs. Turn off to let a request wait for your decision indefinitely.
settings.mcpAutoApproveHint: If you don't respond in time, the request is APPROVED automatically. Use with care: this lets an agent's commit/push/branch actions run without you. Off by default.
settings.mcpApprovalGateDescription: Only MCP tool calls from an agent are gated; actions you take here in the dashboard are never blocked.
settings.mcpApprovalGateFailed: Could not change the MCP Safety Rail settings
settings.desktopNotifyHint: Pop a system notification when a repo falls behind, even when this tab isn't focused.
settings.desktopNotifyBlocked: Notifications are blocked. Allow them for this site in your browser settings.
settings.title: Settings
settings.updateNotifyHint: Check periodically and let you know when a newer version is available. Nothing is installed without your say-so.

shell.allHidden: All repositories are hidden. Turn on "Show hidden" in filters to see them.

signIn.continueLocalHint: Use RepoYeti on this computer without signing in.
signIn.ownerOnly: Only the owner of this daemon can sign in.
signIn.tagline: Your repositories, from anywhere, signed in with Connections.

share.needsRemote: Share links only work while remote access is on. Turn it on above to create one.
share.newHint: Anyone with the link gets in, with no account and no sign-in. Treat it like a password: send it privately, keep it short-lived, and revoke it when you're done.
share.tierViewHint: See repos, uncommitted files, diffs and history. Changes nothing.
share.tierControlHint: Everything in View, plus fetch, pull, push, stage, commit and Smart Commit. Commits are authored as you.
share.scopeAllHint: Includes repos you add later. Off = pick exactly which repos this link can reach; nothing else is even visible.
share.readyOnce: Copy it now: this is the only time it's shown. If you lose it, revoke this link and make another.
share.copyFailed: Couldn't copy. Select the link and copy it manually.
share.expiresInDays: Expires in {n} day | Expires in {n} days
share.expiresInHours: Expires in {n} hour | Expires in {n} hours
share.ephemeralHost: This address changes every time RepoYeti restarts, so links you have already sent will stop working. Set up a named tunnel in Access for an address that stays put.
share.editHint: Changes apply to the existing link. Whoever already has the URL keeps it, and it now grants whatever you set here.
share.staleLink: Sent on an address that has since changed — regenerate to get a working link.
